summary: Post-condition correctly implemented: reconstruction properly aborts when columns are insufficient, never using partial data.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Analyzed abstract state spaces for all variables. Identified two guard conditions that prevent partial data usage. No state anomalies where partial data could be used after reconstruction failure.

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Explored all execution paths through the function. Attempted to find inputs that would cause partial data usage after reconstruction failure. Both failure paths (insufficient columns and reconstruction error) terminate with early return statements.

paths_analyzed.result: Early return with warning, no data used
Early return with error, no data used

paths_analyzed.note: Not a failure case, property does not apply

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Since no vulnerability was found in Phase 2, performed theoretical attack surface analysis. Entry point is P2P network where attacker can withhold columns. However, code correctly handles this scenario with validation layers.
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.data_flow_path: Attacker (malicious peer) → P2P protocol → DataColumnSidecar reception → DB storage (line 1724) → reconstructDataColumns reads from DB (lines 1722-1726) → Guards prevent partial data usage

validation_layers.effectiveness: Complete - enforces early termination when columns.lenu64 < (maxColCount div 2)
Complete - cryptographic verification ensures data integrity
Complete - valueOr pattern ensures no data used on error

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: Attacker can trigger the failure path by withholding columns, but the implementation correctly prevents exploitation through strong validation layers. No vulnerability exists.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Formally proved that the post-condition invariant holds for all execution paths. Two guard conditions (G1: threshold check at line 1730, G2: result validation at line 1740) enforce unconditional termination in all failure scenarios without using partial data.
phase3_invariant_proving.proof_method: Proof by cases covering all execution paths

guard_identified.postcondition: No code executes after line 1732 when triggered

guard_identified.effect: Unconditional termination via return statement (valueOr pattern)
guard_identified.postcondition: No code executes after line 1742 when triggered

phase3_invariant_proving.proof_result: QED - Property holds for all execution paths

phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: No action required. The post-condition is correctly implemented with strong validation layers.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: Component (PeerDAS), EIP (EIP-7594), and entry point (P2P) are all in-scope per Bug Bounty definition. However, formal analysis in Phases 1-3 proved no vulnerability exists. The code correctly prevents use of partial data when reconstruction fails.
